metadata.description: GHG emission intensity benchmark data for CCF platform. All entries are from company-published reports (CSRD ESRS E1-6 or sustainability reports). Sub-sector ranges (min/avg/max) are computed dynamically from the company entries. Source types: csrd_report = first CSRD filing under ESRS; company_sr = company sustainability/environment report (pre-CSRD or non-EU).

scope_definitions.S2: Indirect emissions from purchased energy (market-based used unless noted)
scope_definitions.S1+2+3: Full value chain including supply chain and downstream use-phase

caveats: No public body (IEA, Eurostat, CDP) publishes a ready-made tCO2e/EUR M table by sector. Most values are derived from disclosed absolute figures or from CSRD company reports.
EUR/USD: Global benchmarks use USD. Conversion: 1 tCO2e/USD M ≈ 1.09 tCO2e/EUR M (2022–2023 avg rate 0.92).

Scope 3 inflates totals dramatically for automotive (~98% use-phase), oil & gas (~90% combustion of sold products), food & beverage (~80% agricultural supply chain), and specialty chemicals with Cat 11 sold-product combustion (Arkema, Borealis).
S1+2 is the most comparable metric across sectors and most commonly disclosed. Recommend defaulting to S1+2 for platform comparisons.
Year-to-year comparability is affected by revenue fluctuations (e.g. BASF revenue dropped 21% from 2022 to 2023, inflating intensity).
CSRD-derived benchmarks use FY2023 or FY2024 data depending on company reporting cycle. All are first-year CSRD disclosures; methodology and coverage may improve in subsequent years.
Proprietary databases (Trucost/S&P Sustainable1, MSCI CarbonMetrics) provide GICS-level benchmarks but require subscription.

eu_ets_product_benchmarks.description: EU ETS top-10th-percentile efficiency benchmarks (tCO2e per tonne of product) used for free allowance allocation under EU Reg. 2021/447. These represent best-practice, not sector averages. Average installations emit 2–3x these values. Period: 2021–2025.

raw_kpis.notes: GHG emissions: p.41; Revenue: p.74; FTE: p.96. All GHG emissions are reported in million metric tons CO2e and converted to tCO2e. Revenue is reported in EUR million. FTE refers to full-time equivalents. All data refers to the calendar year 2024.

GHG emissions: p.58, 140; Revenue: p.3, 14, 232, 243, 266; FTE: p.3, 67, 181. All GHG emissions are in tCO2e. Revenue refers to 'Total Operating Income' in EUR million. FTE refers to 'Number of employees (at year end)' including Staci. Page 181 notes that Staci is included in financial statements but not yet fully reflected in the CSRD report for 2024, but will be in the next reporting period. Scope 3 category 'use of sold products' is 0 as the company primarily provides services and packaging products sold have no emissions during their use (p. 139).

GHG emissions: p.126, p.127; Revenue: p.6, p.23, p.36, p.395; FTE: p.6, p.20, p.95, p.179. All emissions are converted from ktCO2e to tCO2e by multiplying by 1,000. Revenue is reported in EUR million. FTE is based on the total headcount at year-end.

GHG emissions: p.180; Revenue: p.5, p.6, p.44; FTE: p.6, p.93. Scope 1, Scope 2 market-based, Scope 2 location-based, and Scope 3 total emissions are from the 'GHG emissions' table on page 180, reported in ktonnes and converted to tCO2e. Scope 3 categories 'Purchased goods and services' and 'Use of sold products' are also from this table. Revenue is 'Net revenue (beia)' from performance highlights and key figures. FTE is 'Average number of employees (FTE)' from key figures and personnel expenses.

GHG emissions: p.92; Revenue: p.183; FTE: p.117, p.198. Scope 2 market-based emissions are 0.0 as KPN's electricity consumption is based on 100% renewable electricity (p.85, p.92). Revenue is reported as 'Total revenues and other income' from the consolidated statement of profit or loss.

GHG emissions: p.191, p.192, p.193, p.194; Revenue: p.4, p.5, p.80, p.137, p.476, p.478; FTE: p.5, p.72, p.159, p.278, p.477. Scope 3 total excludes emissions from 'use of sold products' as per the company's SBTi-approved targets (p.194). Scope 2 market-based emissions are used as preferred. All data refers to the calendar year 2024.

GHG emissions: p.162, 163; Revenue: p.2, 241; FTE: p.2, 185. The reporting period is the calendar year 2024, as confirmed on p.111. Market-based Scope 2 emissions are preferred and used for the `scope2_mb_tco2e` field. Scope 3 total is explicitly stated. Specific Scope 3 categories are also explicitly stated.

GHG emissions: p.131, 133; Revenue: p.2, 42, 102; FTE: p.2, 102, 193. All values are for the 2024 calendar year. Revenue is reported in EUR million. GHG emissions are reported in tCO2e. FTE is reported as headcount.

GHG emissions: p.80; Revenue: p.108; FTE: p.3, p.120. Revenue conversion rate: 1 NOK = 0.086 EUR.

GHG emissions: p. 183; Revenue: p. 9, 164, 280, 303; FTE: p. 195. Scope 1 tCO2e is the sum of direct emissions from stationary combustion sources (242), mobile combustion sources (403), and fugitive emissions (79) for 2025. Scope 2 market-based tCO2e is the sum of indirect emissions from electricity consumption (market-based) (303) and indirect emissions associated with the consumption of steam, heating or cooling (254) for 2025. Scope 3 total tCO2e is explicitly stated as 'TOTAL SCOPES 1, 2 AND 3 (MARKET BASED)' for 2025. FTE represents the 'TOTAL HEADCOUNT WORLDWIDE' for 2025, which includes permanent and non-permanent contracts. Revenue is consistently reported in EUR million.

GHG emissions: p.201; Revenue: p.2, 4, 50, 112, 113, 199, 355; FTE: p.2, 54, 284. All GHG emissions are for the business year 2024/25 (fiscal year ending March 31, 2025). Scope 3 categories 3.6, 3.7, 3.8, 3.10, 3.11, 3.12, 3.13, 3.14, 3.15 are reported as immaterial or not listed with values on p.201. The reporting year is a fiscal year ending March 31, 2025.

GHG emissions: p.318, table "Gross Scopes 1, 2 & 3 and Total GHG Emissions", row "Gross Scope 1 GHG Emissions (tCO2eq)" for Scope 1, row "Gross Scope 2 GHG Emissions (Location-based) (tCO2eq)" for Scope 2 LB, row "Gross Scope 2 GHG Emissions (Market-based) (tCO2eq)" for Scope 2 MB, row "Total Gross Indirect (Scope 3) GHG Emissions (tCO2eq)" for Scope 3 total, row "1 Purchased goods and services (b)" for Scope 3 Cat 1, row "11 Use of sold products" for Scope 3 Cat 11; Revenue: p.219, table "Revenue", row "TOTAL REVENUE"; FTE: p.331, text "The Group’s total number of employees stands at 65,168 people (1) as of December 31, 2025"; All data refers to the year 2025. Scope 2 is market-based as preferred. Revenue is consolidated. FTE is total headcount.

S1+2 ~8 Mt (approx, production operations only). S1+2+3 ~420 Mt — S3 ~99% is Cat11 downstream vehicle use-phase (combustion by customers). Revenue EUR 322.3B. FTE 684,000. All figures approximate from VW Group Sustainability Report 2023.

DHL reports a single total logistics-related emissions figure of 33.27 Mt CO2e covering own fleet, third-party contracted transport, and buildings — broadly equivalent to S1+2+3. S1+2 breakdown not separately available for this entry. 97% renewable electricity (S2 MB near-zero). Revenue and FTE from DHL Group Annual Report 2023.

csrd_company_data.source: DHL Group Environment Report 2023; DHL Group Annual Report 2023

However, the document also states a combined 'Scope 1+2 GHG emissions (market based)' of 34.3 MtCO2e on p.333, and 34 MtCO2e for 'Scope 1+2 from operated facilities' on p.7, p.13, p.20, p.21, p.647. The individual Scope 1 and Scope 2 values provided are the gross figures from the ESRS perimeter tables.

There is a slightly higher revenue figure (€33,239 million) mentioned on p.95 and p.145 for Taxonomy purposes and GHG intensity calculation, but the consolidated income statement figure is used for consistency with financial reporting.

GHG emissions: p.220; Revenue: p.5, p.16, p.57; FTE: p.104, p.183, p.263. All values are for the calendar year 2024. Scope 1, Scope 2 (location-based and market-based), and Scope 3 total emissions are in tCO2e. Revenue is in EUR million. FTE represents total number of employees.

GHG emissions: p.45; Revenue: p.83, p.86; FTE: p.28, p.61. Scope 1, Scope 2 (location-based), Scope 2 (market-based), and Scope 3 (market-based) are reported in million metric tons CO2e on p.45. Scope 3 categories 'Purchased goods and services' (Category 1) and 'Combustion of natural gas sold to end-customers' (Category 11, Use of sold products) are reported in million metric tons CO2e on p.45. FTE is from the 'Core Workforce' table on p.61 and also mentioned on p.28. Revenue is from the 'External Sales' table on p.86 and also mentioned on p.83, stated in € in millions.

GHG emissions: p.38, p.151, p.152; FTE: p.70, p.150, p.197. Reporting year is calendar year ending December 31, 2024 (p.4). Scope 1 emissions (3.08 million tonnes CO2e) and market-based Scope 2 emissions (0.26 million tonnes CO2e) are from the 'Nestlé GHG footprint breakdown by Scope' table on page 38 and confirmed on page 151. Location-based Scope 2 emissions (2.59 million metric tonnes CO2e) are from page 152. The total Scope 3 emissions (81.04 million metric tonnes CO2e) are reported as 'Gross other indirect (Scope 3) GHG emissions' on page 152, representing the total Scope 3 inventory, including categories outside the SBTi target scope (p.37). The report does not provide specific numerical values for individual GHG Protocol categories like 'Purchased goods and services' or 'Use of sold products' in the 2024 data. Revenue is not disclosed in this Non-Financial Statement; the document refers to the 'Nestlé Annual Review' for financial details (p.4). FTE (277,000) refers to the total headcount, including permanent and temporary employees, as stated on pages 70, 150, and 197, and is counted irrespective of full-time or part-time status (p.130).

GHG emissions (Scope 1, Scope 2 market-based, Scope 3 total, Cat 1, Cat 10): p.91 (all values in thousand tonnes, converted to tCO2e). Revenue: p.12, p.54, p.101 (SEK million, converted to EUR million using 1 SEK = 0.087 EUR). FTE: p.12, p.109 (Permanent employees at year end). For 'cat11_use_of_sold_products', 'Category 10: Processing of sold products' was used as the closest available category.

GHG emissions: p.185 (all values are in TeqCO2, which is tCO2e). Scope 2 market-based is preferred. Scope 3 category 'cat11_use_of_sold_products' is explicitly stated as 'N/P' (Not relevant/provided) on p.186. Revenue: p.194 ('Turnover (currency)'), p.278 ('Revenue'). FTE: p.15 ('108,179 EMPLOYEES*'), p.190 ('Groupe Workforce Number'), p.204 ('Total employees'). The document provides both 'Net revenue' (€13,965 million, p.16, p.265, p.278) and 'Revenue' (€16,030 million, p.194, p.278). 'Revenue' is used as it aligns with the 'Turnover (currency)' in the EU Taxonomy table on p.194 and represents a broader measure before pass-through costs, as defined on p.278.

GHG emissions: p.11, p.53, p.78, p.79, p.80; Revenue: p.7, p.10, p.135; FTE: p.11, p.150. Scope 1 GHG emissions (1,000 t CO2e) is 104. Market-based Scope 2 GHG emissions (1,000 t CO2e) is 1. Location-based Scope 2 is not disclosed for 2024. Total Gross indirect (Scope 3) GHG emissions (million t CO2e) is 7.99. Category 1 (Purchased goods and services) is 7.02 million tCO2e. Category 12 (End-of-life treatment of sold products) is 0.07 million tCO2e. Note that the accounting policy on p.80 states 'use of sold products (our products do not emit GHG emissions during their use)', implying zero emissions for the use phase, but the table on p.79 provides a value for 'End-of-life treatment of sold products' which is used for cat11. Revenue is 17,295 mEUR. FTE is the average number of employees. All data points refer to the calendar year 2024.

GHG emissions: p.109; Revenue: p.5, p.21, p.30, p.34, p.340; FTE: p.164, p.304, p.341. The reporting year is a calendar year (January 1 to December 31, 2024) as confirmed on p.326. For Scope 3, 'Use of sold products' (Cat 11) is reported as n/a on p.110, with a footnote explaining that PUMA does not cause any direct use phase emissions and reports indirect use phase emissions as n/a as it is optional under GHG protocol.

GHG emissions: p.236; Revenue: p.174; FTE: p.237. Scope 3 category 'Use of sold products' is stated as 'Not applicable' on p.274. Revenue of €3,252 million from contracts with customers (p.174) was chosen over 'Total revenue including interest' of €3,275 million (p.160) as it represents core business revenue.

GHG emissions: p.166; Revenue: p.18, p.42; FTE: p.96, p.123, p.240. The reporting year 2024 refers to the fiscal year 1 February 2024 – 31 January 2025. Scope 2 market-based is preferred over location-based. Category 11 for Scope 3 is used as requested.

GHG emissions: p.293; Revenue: p.24; FTE: p.26. Revenue is reported in SEK million and converted to EUR million using the provided rate (1 SEK = 0.087 EUR). Scope 3 category 'Use of sold products' is not reported as it is not calculated by the company (p.292).

GHG emissions: p.160; Revenue: p.193; FTE: p.215. All emissions are converted from million metric tons to tCO2e (x 1,000,000). GHG emissions: p.171; Revenue: p.27; FTE: p.40, p.191. Scope 1, Scope 2 (location-based and market-based), and Scope 3 total emissions are for the Enea Group in 2024. For Scope 2, the value 397,077.15 tCO2e (labeled 'Gross market-based Scope 2 GHG emissions') is used as location-based as it aligns with the 'Total GHG emissions (location-based method)' when combined with Scope 1 and Scope 3. The value 558,555.92 tCO2e (labeled 'Gross Scope 2 GHG emissions measured using the market-based method') is used as market-based.
